commit_message:
Regenerate test certificates (#147819)

Regenerates expired test certificates to fix JDK 26 SSL failures that JDK 26 introduced indirectly with `X509KeyManagerCertChecking`. When an expired certificate is encountered, the validity check calls Date.toString() on the expiry date that fails with `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException`.  More info in https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/146645#issuecomment-4293971360

- Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/146645
- Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/147528
- Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/147529
- Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/147530
- Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/147168
- Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/146974
- Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/146973

commit_message:
[ESQL] Fix Windows path separators in fixture loading (#147148)

Path.relativize() returns backslash-separated paths on Windows,
causing fixture blob keys like `warehouse/standalone\employees.orc`.
Test queries use forward slashes, producing a key mismatch and 404
for every remote storage backend (HTTP, S3, GCS, Azure).

This explains the 100% OrcFormatSpecIT (and other format) failure
rate on Windows CI while LOCAL tests pass: the mock server stores
the blob under `standalone\employees.orc` but the query looks up
`standalone/employees.orc`.

Fix: normalize backslashes to forward slashes in
`FixtureUtils.forEachFixtureEntryFromFilesystem`.

Also unmutes all OrcFormatSpecIT tests.

Closes #146982

commit_message:
[ESQL] Fix Windows path separators in fixture loading (#147148)

Path.relativize() returns backslash-separated paths on Windows,
causing fixture blob keys like `warehouse/standalone\employees.orc`.
Test queries use forward slashes, producing a key mismatch and 404
for every remote storage backend (HTTP, S3, GCS, Azure).

This explains the 100% OrcFormatSpecIT (and other format) failure
rate on Windows CI while LOCAL tests pass: the mock server stores
the blob under `standalone\employees.orc` but the query looks up
`standalone/employees.orc`.

Fix: normalize backslashes to forward slashes in
`FixtureUtils.forEachFixtureEntryFromFilesystem`.

Also unmutes all OrcFormatSpecIT tests.

Closes #146982

[ESQL] Fix Windows path separators in fixture loading (#147148)

Path.relativize() returns backslash-separated paths on Windows,
causing fixture blob keys like `warehouse/standalone\employees.orc`.
Test queries use forward slashes, producing a key mismatch and 404
for every remote storage backend (HTTP, S3, GCS, Azure).

This explains the 100% OrcFormatSpecIT (and other format) failure
rate on Windows CI while LOCAL tests pass: the mock server stores
the blob under `standalone\employees.orc` but the query looks up
`standalone/employees.orc`.

Fix: normalize backslashes to forward slashes in
`FixtureUtils.forEachFixtureEntryFromFilesystem`.

Also unmutes all OrcFormatSpecIT tests.

commit_message:
[ESQL] Fix Windows path separators in fixture loading (#147148)

Path.relativize() returns backslash-separated paths on Windows,
causing fixture blob keys like `warehouse/standalone\employees.orc`.
Test queries use forward slashes, producing a key mismatch and 404
for every remote storage backend (HTTP, S3, GCS, Azure).

This explains the 100% OrcFormatSpecIT (and other format) failure
rate on Windows CI while LOCAL tests pass: the mock server stores
the blob under `standalone\employees.orc` but the query looks up
`standalone/employees.orc`.

Fix: normalize backslashes to forward slashes in
`FixtureUtils.forEachFixtureEntryFromFilesystem`.

Also unmutes all OrcFormatSpecIT tests.

Closes #146982

[ESQL] Fix Windows path separators in fixture loading (#147148)

Path.relativize() returns backslash-separated paths on Windows,
causing fixture blob keys like `warehouse/standalone\employees.orc`.
Test queries use forward slashes, producing a key mismatch and 404
for every remote storage backend (HTTP, S3, GCS, Azure).

This explains the 100% OrcFormatSpecIT (and other format) failure
rate on Windows CI while LOCAL tests pass: the mock server stores
the blob under `standalone\employees.orc` but the query looks up
`standalone/employees.orc`.

Fix: normalize backslashes to forward slashes in
`FixtureUtils.forEachFixtureEntryFromFilesystem`.

Also unmutes all OrcFormatSpecIT tests.

commit_message:
[ESQL] Fix Windows path separators in fixture loading (#147148)

Path.relativize() returns backslash-separated paths on Windows,
causing fixture blob keys like `warehouse/standalone\employees.orc`.
Test queries use forward slashes, producing a key mismatch and 404
for every remote storage backend (HTTP, S3, GCS, Azure).

This explains the 100% OrcFormatSpecIT (and other format) failure
rate on Windows CI while LOCAL tests pass: the mock server stores
the blob under `standalone\employees.orc` but the query looks up
`standalone/employees.orc`.

Fix: normalize backslashes to forward slashes in
`FixtureUtils.forEachFixtureEntryFromFilesystem`.

Also unmutes all OrcFormatSpecIT tests.

Closes #146982

commit_message:
S3BlobContainerRetriesTests.testWriteLargeBlobStreaming: tolerate more than one retry (#147194)

This test tries to force exactly one retry per part, but it is possible to get more than
one if the server accepts the upload but the client doesn't receive the success response
before its read timeout kicks in. Before this change, if that happened then the server
could double-count the bytes of the additional upload. This change tracks the bytes of
each part upload and counts only the first success.

Fixes #147082

commit_message:
ESQL: More explicit test for unresolved promql (#147302)

Promql's unresolved functions turn into one of four different "shapes"
of logical plan after the function is resolved. At analyer time. The
UnresolvedPromqlFunction is how we ask the analyzer to make that change,
but it's upsetting EsqlNodeSubclassTests because it's "unresolved" AND a
LogicalPlan. And EsqlNodeSubclassTests only supports unresolved
*Expression* subclasses! This adds an explicit filter out for this
strange case.

Closes #147164

commit_message:
Regenerate test certificates (#147819)

Regenerates expired test certificates to fix JDK 26 SSL failures that JDK 26 introduced indirectly with `X509KeyManagerCertChecking`. When an expired certificate is encountered, the validity check calls Date.toString() on the expiry date that fails with `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException`.  More info in https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/146645#issuecomment-4293971360

- Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/146645
- Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/147528

- Resolves https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/147530

Fix race in FsBlobContainer.moveBlobAtomic by replacing move op with hard link (#147405)
